titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the School of Art, Yale University, Lectures and Presentations
School of Art, Yale University, Lectures and Presentations
titlestmt.author: compiled by Daniel Hartwig

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday November 7, 2020 at 11:07

did.unittitle: School of Art, Yale University, lectures and presentations
did.abstract: The materials consist of audiorecordings of lectures and presentations at the Yale School of Art.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 20-H (YRG 20-H), Audio-visual materials and memorabilia documenting the Yale School of Art

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The materials were transferred from the School of Art, 2008

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research

Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright for materials authored or otherwise produced as official business of Yale University is retained by Yale University. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: School of Art, Yale University, Lectures and Presentations (RU 1082). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Yale University records are arranged and described at the accession level by the creating office. The University Archives creates collection level descriptive records, but typically does no further arrangement and description at the accession level

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The materials consist of audiorecordings of lectures and presentations at the Yale School of Art

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

did.unittitle: "Painting: Textuality and Borrowing: Poussin, Manet and Picasso"
"Painting: Omnipotence and the Gaze: Ingres, The Wolf Man and Picasso"
Berthot, Jake, "Speaking on His Own Work at Norfolk"
Ryan, Richard, "Speaking on His Own Work at the Yale Summer School of Art at Norfolk"
Koons, Jeff and Ron Jones, "Conversations at Yale"
Holzer, Jenny, R. Jones, and R. Flood

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

did.unittitle: Lawder, Standish, Runaway , Corridor , Dangling Participle , and others
HSW talks on MDS, Yale Club of New Haven
